paper_title: Calculating CO2 from alkenone δ13C values: the CO2(εp−alk) proxy

methods.description: This method recalculates CO2(εp−alk) records using a consistent approach to facilitate fair comparison between sites and with ice core records. The approach is based on Bidigare et al. (1997) and removes additional corrections used in original publications.

steps.description: Recalculate all CO2(εp−alk) records using a consistent approach based on Bidigare et al. (1997).
steps.searchable_summary: Recalculate CO2(εp−alk) records using Bidigare et al. (1997) method

steps.description: Estimate the 'b' term using modern-day surface [PO3−4].

methods.method_name: Calculation of Isotopic Composition and CO2 Concentration
methods.description: This method involves calculating the isotopic composition of dissolved inorganic carbon (DIC) and CO2, and subsequently calculating εp and [CO2](aq) using isotopic fractionation and physiological factors.

steps.description: Estimate the isotopic composition of DIC using δ13C value of planktic foraminifera and temperature-dependent fractionation.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate isotopic composition of DIC using δ13C and temperature

steps.description: Calculate the value of the carbon isotopic composition of CO2(g).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate carbon isotopic composition of CO2(g)

steps.description: Calculate δ13C CO2(aq) using the relationship determined by Mook et al. (1974).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate δ13C CO2(aq) using Mook et al. (1974) relationship

steps.description: Calculate εp and from that [CO2](aq) using isotopic fractionation during carbon fixation (εf) and b.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate εp and [CO2](aq) using isotopic fractionation and b

steps.description: Calculate atmospheric CO2 concentration from [CO2](aq) using Henry's law.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate atmospheric CO2 from [CO2](aq) using Henry's law
